subject: ap-chemistry
title: Thermochemistry: Recycling vs. Extracting Aluminum
stimulus: Aluminum metal can be recycled from scrap metal by melting the metal to evaporate impurities.

parts.prompt:
Calculate the amount of heat needed to purify 1.00 mole of Al originally at 298 K by melting it. The
melting point of Al is 933 K. The molar heat capacity of Al is 24 J/(mol⋅K), and the heat of fusion
of Al is 10.7 kJ/mol.

scoring_points.description: For correct heating step ($q = nC_p\Delta T$): 1 point

required_elements: $q_1 = nC_p\Delta T = (1.00\text{ mol})(24\text{ J/mol·K})(933 - 298\text{ K}) = 15240\text{ J} = 15.2\text{ kJ}$

wrong_examples: Using ΔT = 933 K (treating melting point as the temperature change rather than the final temperature).
common_traps: $\Delta T = T_{final} - T_{initial} = 933 - 298 = 635$ K, not 933 K.

scoring_points.description: For correct fusion step and total heat: 1 point
wrong_examples: Not adding the heat of fusion to the sensible heat — both steps must be included.
common_traps: Two steps required: (1) heat solid from 298 K to melting point (sensible heat), THEN (2) melt at constant temperature (latent heat of fusion).

parts.prompt:
The equation for the overall process of extracting Al from $Al_2O_3$ is shown below. Which requires less
energy, recycling existing Al or extracting Al from $Al_2O_3$? Justify your answer with a calculation.
$Al_2O_3$(s) → 2 Al(s) + $\frac{3}{2}$ $O_2$(g)  $\Delta H°_{rxn}$ = +1675 kJ/mol
parts.scoring_notes: The extraction reaction produces 2 mol Al per 1675 kJ, so per mole Al = 1675/2 = 837.5 kJ/mol Al. Recycling costs ~26 kJ/mol Al (from part a). Recycling requires far less energy (26 kJ vs. 838 kJ per mole of Al).

scoring_points.description: For identifying recycling as requiring less energy with numerical comparison: 1 point

required_elements: Extraction: $1675/2 = 837.5$ kJ per mol Al; Recycling: ~26 kJ per mol Al.
alternatives.correct_example:
Extraction of Al from Al$_2$O$_3$: $1675\text{ kJ} / 2\text{ mol Al} = 837.5\text{ kJ/mol Al}$.
Recycling requires only ~26 kJ/mol Al (from part a).
Recycling requires far less energy per mole of Al ($\approx 26$ kJ vs. $\approx 838$ kJ).

wrong_examples: Extraction requires less — 1675 kJ to extract 2 mol from Al$_2$O$_3$ is enormously more than 26 kJ to melt 1 mol of Al.
common_traps: The extraction equation produces 2 mol Al. Divide 1675 kJ by 2 to get energy per mole of Al.

scoring_points.description: For correct energy comparison per mole of Al: 1 point

required_elements: Recycling energy per mol Al ≈ 26 kJ (from part a).
Extraction energy per mol Al = 1675/2 ≈ 838 kJ.
alternatives.correct_example: Energy comparison: Extraction needs 837.5 kJ/mol Al; recycling needs ~26 kJ/mol Al. Recycling uses about 3% as much energy as primary production from Al$_2$O$_3$.

wrong_examples: Comparing 1675 kJ to 26 kJ without adjusting for 2 mol Al produced in extraction.
common_traps: Must normalize to per mole of Al before comparing: extraction equation gives 2 mol Al per 1675 kJ.
